task_completion.description: Vital Recorder is open with VitalDB case 6 loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Vital Recorder application window is visible, and a file indicating case 6 (e.g., 0006.vital) is loaded in the interface.
The specific track 'Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2' is visible in the Vital Recorder track list and its waveform/data is displayed in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates the timeline to examine the track
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the timeline at different positions, or zooming in/out, indicating the agent is actively scanning the recording for dropouts.

task_completion.description: A text editor is open for the dropout report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like Notepad) is open, and the window title or save dialog indicates the file is being saved as 'dropout_report.txt' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\'.

task_completion.description: The report contains the correct header information
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows the exact header: 'SIGNAL DROPOUT REPORT', 'Case: VitalDB #6', 'Track: Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2', and 'Date of Audit:' with a date.

task_completion.description: Dropout episodes are listed in the specified format
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file includes 'Total dropout episodes found: [N]' and lists episodes with 'Start:', 'End:', and 'Duration:' (or shows 0 episodes if none were found).

task_completion.description: Total dropout time and signal availability are included
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom of the text file contains the lines 'Total dropout time: [seconds]' and 'Signal availability: [percentage]%'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Vital Recorder GUI to examine the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent actually opened and interacted with the Vital Recorder application, rather than using a Python script or terminal tool to extract the data and bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The report was created in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or pasting the report into a text editor GUI, rather than using a script to silently generate the text file in the background.

integrity.description: The agent spent time examining the track before writing the report
integrity.visual_evidence: There is evidence of the agent looking through the Vital Recorder timeline before or while filling out the dropout episodes, rather than instantly producing the final report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: VitalDB case 6 is a real surgical recording. The vital file is typically named 0006.vital and contains the Solar8000/PLETH_SPO2 track (Percutaneous oxygen saturation, numeric, %). The agent must examine this specific track in the Vital Recorder software to find dropouts (flat-lines at zero or missing data) lasting 10 seconds or longer.
